The '''DC0052 speeder''', nicknamed "'''Intergalactic'''," was an [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] utilized by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] in the waning days of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Developed by [[Daystar Craft]], the two-[[Meter/Legends|meter]] long vehicle featured a top speed of eight hundred [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] per [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] and could travel over two thousand kilometers uninterrupted. The craft was inexpensive to maintain and featured a special "drop-deployment" panel that allowed [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] to enter the heat of battle swiftly while letting the DC0052 land itself with [[Autopilot/Legends|autopilot]]. Sixteen of the vehicles were kept and maintained by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]].

==Characteristics==
The DC0052 [[Airspeeder/Legends|speeder]] was a cheap, short-range craft and could travel over two thousand [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] without interruption. It featured a mid-altitude ceiling of one hundred and seventy kilometers, with a top speed of eight hundred kilometers per [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]]. The craft's aerodynamic surfaces allowed it to travel swiftly across urban areas, while being propelled by dual [[Sono-class thrust pod|''Sono''-class thrust pods]].<ref name="db" />

To aid it in its movement, the DC0052 also featured four [[Daystar Craft]] [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] generators, which were powered by the unit's durable power supply unit. Unique to the DC0052 was a "drop-deployment" panel. The panel would allow a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who had set the craft to land on [[Autopilot/Legends|autopilot]] to drop out from underneath the craft and enter a situation with due haste.<ref name="db" /> Designed in the shape of a flying wing, the DC0052 had a small, one-being [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] located centrally.<ref name="rots" />

==History==
[[File:DC0052Speeders-SWTCG-RotS.jpg|thumb|left|180px|DC0052 speeders in flight]]
Manufactured by [[Daystar Craft]], the inexpensive DC0052 speeder was exclusively used by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] in the waning days of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as personal [[Transport/Legends|transports]] to ferry [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] from one location to another. It was a successor to the earlier [[DC0040 speeder]], which had been touted and advertised as "Intergalactic" by Daystar's marketers. Though the "Intergalactic" moniker was never formally applied to the DC0052, it was later associated with the craft. DC0052s were primarily found on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] during this [[Time/Legends|time]], though they were also used on other [[Planet/Legends|worlds]].<ref name="db" />

The [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] maintained a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of sixteen DC0052 speeders, which they used to ferry themselves all over the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]]. In doing so, the Council avoided the need to utilize the planet's public transportation system or to use the already overworked [[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|Delta-7 ''Aethersprite''-class light interceptors]] and [[Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|Eta-2 ''Actis''-class interceptors]] in the role. The DC0052's range gave the Jedi access to many of the planet's locations without the need to enter [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]].<ref name="db" /> Some of the units were emblazoned with the insignia of the [[Open Circle Fleet/Legends|Open Circle Fleet]].<ref name="rots" /> Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Ahsoka Tano/Legends|Ahsoka Tano]] used the speeders several times on Coruscant<ref name="Sphere of Influence">{{TCW|Sphere of Influence}}</ref><ref name="Assassin">{{TCW|Assassin (episode)}}</ref> around [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]].<ref name="SW Annual 2011">''[[Star Wars Annual 2011]]''</ref> Tano used one of the transports to find her Master, [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], and [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] at one of the landing pads on the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]]—from where Amidala was preparing to depart from the capital aboard her [[J-type diplomatic barge/Legends|J-type diplomatic barge]]—to deliver some news to them.<ref name="Sphere of Influence" /> Tano also used a DC0052 speeder to travel from the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] to meet up with Amidala at a landing pad, from where the Senator was about to leave Coruscant on another mission.<ref name="Assassin" /> Similarly Tano also used one of these speeders to travel to a republic prison to speak with [[Letta Turmond]] after being requested by her, and when Tano was accused of murdering Turmond she attempt to return to the speeder to escape from the prison, but it was destroyed by a nearby [[Turret/Legends|turret]]. In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Skywalker utilized one of the vehicles to ferry himself from the Jedi Temple to the [[Republic Executive Building/Legends|Republic Executive Building]], where the [[Showdown on Coruscant|attempted arrest]] of [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] was taking place. Later that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], following the [[Declaration of a New Order]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] used one of the vehicles to reach Amidala's [[Naboo Queen (restaurant)|apartment]], where he met with the Senator.<ref name="rots" />

==Behind the scenes==
The DC0052 speeder first appeared in [[2005]] in [[George Lucas]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']], though it went unnamed in the film. It was later named and elaborated on through the [[StarWars.com]] [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] feature, "[[What's The Story?]]." The vehicle's backstory and specifications were written by [[Nathan Butler]] (''[[Equals & Opposites|Equals and Opposites]]'') and "[[The Dark Moose]]" ([[Unstable Terrain Artillery Transport/Legends|Unstable Terrain Artillery Transport]] in the [[Databank (original)|Databank]]). The name had already been established in the submission sheet; "The Dark Moose" provided most of the information, although his nickname for the vehicle, the "Deuce," did not make it to the final version of the entry.<ref name="blog">{{Blog|moosepoodo|81|What's the Story? Nah - What's the Holdup?}}</ref> The vehicle was later given an entry in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', using information from the speeder's Databank entry.<ref name="CSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> In [[2010]], over five years since its original creation,<ref name="Sphere of Influence Ep Guide">{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode304.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Sphere of Influence}}</ref><ref name="Assassin Ep Guide">{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode307.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Assassin}}</ref> the DC0052 speeder was featured in the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series' [[The Clone Wars: Season Three|third season]], first in the episode "[[Sphere of Influence]]"<ref name="Sphere of Influence" /> and later in the episode "[[Assassin (episode)|Assassin]]."<ref name="Assassin" />
